items.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

This is a sub-issue of [#53864 – Azure Mgmt Generator migration](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864), tracking the **MPG (Management Plane Generator) migration for the Azure Maps management SDK** from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec-based generation.

> ⚠️ The issue body is currently empty — details about scope, progress, or blockers have not yet been filled in.

<details>
<summary>📋 Context & Background</summary>

- The parent tracking issue [#53864](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864) describes a broad effort to migrate all Azure management-plane SDKs to the new TypeSpec/mgmt-generator pipeline.
- The Maps SDK lives under `sdk/maps/` and contains `Azure.ResourceManager.Maps` (and possibly data-plane packages).
- Completed RP migrations are tracked in [#55080](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/55080).

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks Checklist</summary>

- [ ] Identify the TypeSpec spec location for Azure Maps (`specification/maps/` in `azure-rest-api-specs`)
- [ ] Verify `tsp-location.yaml` exists or create it under `sdk/maps/Azure.ResourceManager.Maps/`
- [ ] Run `azsdk_package_generate_code` (or `Generate.ps1`) to regenerate from TypeSpec
- [ ] Fix any breaking changes (use `CodeGenSuppress`, `CodeGenMember`, partial classes, `@`@clientName`` as needed)
- [ ] Run `Export-API.ps1` to update API listings
- [ ] Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test` — resolve any failures
- [ ] Run pre-commit checks (`dotnet format`, snippet updates)
- [ ] Open a PR and ensure CI is green

</details>

<details>
<summary>🔧 Debugging / Migration Tips</summary>

- Use the `mpg-sdk-migration` skill for guided migration steps.
- Use the `mitigate-breaking-changes` skill for SDK-side customization patterns (`CodeGenType`, `CodeGenSuppress`, `@`@clientName``, `hierarchyBuilding`).
- If you encounter `CS0103 'scope does not exist'` errors in generated sync methods, this is a known generator bug — check the mgmt emitter for the `using DiagnosticScope scope = ...` line in sync method bodies.
- If `@`@clientName`` on union types causes broken `global::.TypeName` in `ModelFactory`, use `CodeGenSuppress` + a custom method.
- Enable `enable-wire-path-attribute: true` in `tspconfig.yaml` if the existing package used `WirePathAttribute`.

</details>

<details>
<summary>📚 Relevant Resources</summary>

- [AGENTS.md – Azure SDK for .NET](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/AGENTS.md)
- [MPG SDK Migration skill](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mpg-sdk-migration/)
- [Mitigate Breaking Changes skill](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mitigate-breaking-changes/)
- [Local SDK Workflow instructions](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/common/instructions/azsdk-tools/local-sdk-workflow.instructions.md)
- [Parent tracking issue #53864](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864)
- [Completed migrations tracking #55080](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/55080)

</details>
